name: Random Forest
applicable_scenarios: tabular classification or regression with mixed feature types
strong baseline that needs minimal feature preprocessing
when feature importance / partial dependence is useful
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier, RandomForestRegressor

def fit_rf_classifier(X, y, n_estimators=300, seed=0):
    model = RandomForestClassifier(
        n_estimators=n_estimators, n_jobs=-1, random_state=seed,
    )
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model

def fit_rf_regressor(X, y, n_estimators=300, seed=0):
    model = RandomForestRegressor(
        n_estimators=n_estimators, n_jobs=-1, random_state=seed,
    )
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model
typical_cases: biological / clinical classification from tabular measurements
variable importance studies in ecological data
common_pitfalls: many trees use lots of memory; watch n_estimators * max_depth
default tree depth overfits small datasets; tune max_depth / min_samples_leaf
feature importance is biased toward high-cardinality / continuous features — prefer permutation importance
